author: @andyjones4673
text: Only just come across this channel and it's great. So much of interest, hoping to find something on how to make a cheap emergency  water filter and understand what they actually achieve. Also are these Brita type jugs really worth the while or not.

replies.author: @Buzzhumma
replies.text: Yes they great but filter your water if needed in a bucket full of sand  with hole in bottom and then boil water and then filter in brita. It depends what water you start with !

replies.author: @andyjones4673
replies.text: @Buzzhumma I watched his desalination playlist which was interesting and the rocket stoves look awesome. I wondered initially about getting rid of the Brita style filters as they don't appear to really do much as per their specifications, just taste really. Unfortunately our water is supplied by profit seeking,corner cutting private companies owned by who knows who. By the time they get pulled up for anything like bad practice etc the damage us already done(my water jug starts getting green residue in its crevices within 14 days which it never used to and it is being well cleaned even between the 4 weekly filter change) . I read many years ago that many of the hiking/survival filtration devices removed bacteria, protozoa,heavy metals and even corona viruses. The latter is no longer within the descriptions any more but I'm sure reverse osmosis sorts that one out. I guess I'm asking if there's a better final stage filtration from tap to glass other than these Brita filters. I assume by boiling water in the kettle it kills bacteria and protozoa but does not remove chemicals and the other stuff.

replies.author: @derrick_v
replies.text: @andyjones4673 your precious govt is in cahoots with those supposedly evil private businesses. Private business isn't the problem, it's government.

replies.author: @dont.ripfuller6587
